author: mark
text:
Add a bare-hub variant to the File/Folder Layout diagram

The layout diagram showed only the ordinary clone — project files on a working tree, .balls/ 'gitignored on main'. A reader who internalized the bare central-hub section then hit a diagram that contradicts it: a bare hub has no working tree, .balls/ and .balls-worktrees/ are loose store dirs next to the bare gitdir, and nothing is gitignored because there is no checked-out main.

Split the section into a labelled 'Ordinary clone' block (unchanged) and a new 'Bare central hub' block, with a prose paragraph spelling out what differs (no project working tree, the loose store is not a tracked working set, the per-task worktrees are the only real working trees) and what does not (state worktree, .balls/tasks symlink, orphan branch are identical). Reconciled the .gitignore-entries subsection: those entries only govern the ordinary clone; a bare hub has no main to ignore from.
